message:
I was tracking a storm rolling in last week, phone in hand, checking radar updates... and at like 60% battery, it just died. Turns out I'd been scrolling for an hour and completely spaced on charging it overnight. Suddenly I'm standing there in the dark, no weather alerts, no flashlight app, nothing.

It sounds silly, but it reminded me that our phones are kind of the nerve center of how we handle emergencies these days. We rely on them for alerts, maps, calling for help, even just staying calm with information.

So now I'm a lot more intentional: I charge devices the afternoon *before* a storm rolls in, I've got a portable battery tucked in the emergency kit, and I actually know where my old phone chargers are (you'd be shocked how often they work).

Do you have a backup charging setup, or is that one of those things you're planning to sort out? 😊
